Keywords: tab bar bottom navigation primary sections
When to Use: Primary app sections (2-5 tabs); always-visible destinations; equal-weight sections
When to Avoid: More than 5 tabs; hierarchical content; infrequently used sections
iOS Convention: UITabBarController; tabs at bottom; SF Symbols icons; title + icon; max 5 tabs
Android Convention: Bottom Navigation Bar; 3-5 destinations; Material Icons; label visible; min 3 tabs
Cross-Platform Note: React Navigation BottomTabNavigator; Flutter BottomNavigationBar / NavigationBar
Accessibility: Each tab must have accessibilityLabel; badge values announced; active tab state announced

Keywords: stack push navigation drill-down hierarchy
When to Use: Hierarchical content; drill-down flows; detail views; multi-step flows
When to Avoid: Peer-level navigation; equal-weight sections that users switch between frequently
iOS Convention: UINavigationController; back button top-left; swipe-right to go back; large title on root
Android Convention: Jetpack Navigation; top app bar with back arrow; system back button/gesture; Material You
Cross-Platform Note: React Navigation Stack; Flutter Navigator 2.0 / GoRouter; NavigationStack (SwiftUI)
Accessibility: Back button must be accessible; screen title announced on push; swipe-back has no a11y alternative — add back button

Keywords: drawer sidebar menu hamburger navigation
When to Use: Secondary navigation; many destinations; settings/profile access; less frequent destinations
When to Avoid: Primary navigation (prefer tab bar); if 5+ main sections consider tabs first
iOS Convention: Not native iOS — use custom or avoid; prefer tab bar or contextual menus
Android Convention: NavigationDrawer (MD3); hamburger menu in top app bar; swipe from left edge to open
Cross-Platform Note: React Navigation Drawer; Flutter Drawer widget; NavigationDrawer in Compose
Accessibility: Drawer must be reachable via button (not gesture only); announce open/close state; focus trapped inside when open

Keywords: modal full screen overlay dismiss
When to Use: Complete task before returning; immersive content (camera media); login/auth flows
When to Avoid: Non-critical information; simple confirmations; use bottom sheet instead for partial content
iOS Convention: UIViewController presented modally; UISheetPresentationController; dismiss via X or swipe down
Android Convention: Full-screen dialog (MD3); dismiss via back button/gesture; optional close icon top-right
Cross-Platform Note: React Navigation Modal stack; Flutter showDialog or MaterialPageRoute with fullscreenDialog
Accessibility: Screen reader should announce modal opened; focus moves to modal; background interaction blocked; ESC/back announces dismiss

Keywords: bottom sheet sheet modal partial overlay
When to Use: Secondary actions; additional info without leaving context; media player; filter panel
When to Avoid: Primary actions (use FAB); single most important action (use button); deep navigation
iOS Convention: UISheetPresentationController (.medium .large detents); grab bar; swipe to dismiss
Android Convention: ModalBottomSheet (MD3); persistent or modal; drag handle optional; scrim dismisses
Cross-Platform Note: React Native @gorhom/bottom-sheet; Flutter showModalBottomSheet; Compose ModalBottomSheet
Accessibility: Accessible via VoiceOver swipe; announce sheet as 'popup'; focus moves to first interactive element in sheet

Keywords: search search bar overlay fullscreen
When to Use: App-wide search; filtering large lists; content discovery
When to Avoid: Always-visible search bar (embed in nav bar instead); single-screen filtering (use inline)
iOS Convention: UISearchController; embedded in nav bar or full-screen; activates on tap; clear button
Android Convention: SearchBar (MD3); expands to full top bar; activated on icon tap; clear X when typing
Cross-Platform Note: React Native SearchBar pattern; Flutter SearchDelegate or SliverAppBar pinned search
Accessibility: Search field must be labeled; results count announced; clear button labeled 'Clear search'

Keywords: deep link universal link routing url
When to Use: External links (email notifications marketing); re-engagement; cross-app routing
When to Avoid: First-time user flow (show onboarding); empty/loading state
iOS Convention: Universal Links (apple-app-site-association); scene(_:openURLContexts:); handle in SceneDelegate
Android Convention: App Links (Digital Asset Links JSON); intent-filter in AndroidManifest; handle in Activity
Cross-Platform Note: React Navigation linkingOptions; GoRouter uri redirect; useDeepLinking Flutter
Accessibility: Deep links must not bypass required auth; maintain back stack; handle invalid URLs gracefully

Keywords: split view master detail ipad large screen landscape
When to Use: iPad and large-screen layouts; master-detail patterns; productivity apps
When to Avoid: Phone-only apps; content that requires full width; games
iOS Convention: UISplitViewController; sidebar + detail column; .doubleColumn or .tripleColumn style
Android Convention: Adaptive layout with NavigationRail + detail pane; fold display support
Cross-Platform Note: React Navigation split; Flutter NavigatorRail; adaptive layout pattern
Accessibility: Both panels must be reachable; column headers announced; collapsed/expanded state announced

Keywords: nested tab tab within tab secondary tabs
When to Use: Section-within-section content; multiple dimensions of categorization
When to Avoid: More than 2 levels of tab nesting; when flat alternatives exist; confuses users quickly
iOS Convention: Avoid nesting UITabBarController; use segment control inside a tab instead
Android Convention: Top Tabs inside a bottom nav tab via ViewPager2; use sparingly; max 2 levels
Cross-Platform Note: React Navigation nested stacks inside bottom tabs; Flutter tabs inside tab
Accessibility: Each level of tabs must be labeled distinctly; avoid duplicate tab labels across levels

Keywords: top tab horizontal scrollable tab swipe
When to Use: Peer-level content categories; swipeable between sections (social feeds media)
When to Avoid: Primary app navigation (prefer bottom nav on mobile); more than 7 top tabs
iOS Convention: Not native iOS — use UISegmentedControl for 2-3 options; TabView with PageTabViewStyle
Android Convention: TabRow / ScrollableTabRow (MD3); swipe between pages; indicator underline
Cross-Platform Note: React Native react-native-tab-view; Flutter TabBar + TabBarView
Accessibility: Tab items must be labeled; selected state announced; swipe gesture navigable by accessibility

Keywords: navigation rail side rail large screen tablet
When to Use: Tablet/large phone landscape; 3-7 destinations; when screen width > 600dp
When to Avoid: Small phone screens; narrow viewports; more than 7 destinations
iOS Convention: Not a native iOS component; use sidebar on iPad (UISplitViewController sidebar)
Android Convention: NavigationRail (MD3); left side on landscape tablet; icons with optional labels
Cross-Platform Note: Flutter NavigationRail; Compose NavigationRail; adaptive: bottom nav on phone → rail on tablet
Accessibility: Tab order follows rail items top-to-bottom; FAB in rail header should be first in focus order

Keywords: swipe page pager horizontal scroll pages
When to Use: Onboarding screens; media galleries; flat peer content (feeds stories)
When to Avoid: Navigating hierarchical content; when page count > 7; if back navigation is ambiguous
iOS Convention: UIPageViewController; page control dots; scroll indicator; avoid with tab bar overlap
Android Convention: ViewPager2 (MD3); HorizontalPager in Compose; page indicator dots optional
Cross-Platform Note: React Navigation tab with swipeEnabled; Flutter PageView; Swipeable (RN Reanimated)
Accessibility: Page change announced; page count communicated; swipe has no keyboard alternative — provide next/prev buttons

Keywords: settings preferences drill-down sections
When to Use: App settings and preferences; user profile; account management
When to Avoid: Dynamic content; primary features; items users access daily
iOS Convention: UITableViewController grouped style; drill-down push nav; toggles + selection cells
Android Convention: Settings screen with grouped list (MD3 List); switch rows; drill-down for sub-sections
Cross-Platform Note: React Native SettingsScreen pattern; Flutter ListTile navigation; GoRouter nested routes
Accessibility: Section headers announced; toggle state read aloud; destructive actions require confirmation

Keywords: context menu long press popup quick action
When to Use: Quick actions on items (edit delete share); item-level secondary actions
When to Avoid: Primary action (use button); navigation to unrelated screens
iOS Convention: UIContextMenuInteraction; UIMenu; preview peek; activates on long press
Android Convention: Context menu (MD3); anchor to item; PopupMenu; long press trigger
Cross-Platform Note: React Native ContextMenu; Pressable onLongPress; Flutter ContextMenuButtonItem
Accessibility: Accessible via 'Actions' in VoiceOver; menu items announced; dismissed on Escape/back

Keywords: tab badge notification unread count indicator
When to Use: Unread count; new content notification; pending action count
When to Avoid: Decorative use; counts that update excessively; showing counts > 999
iOS Convention: UITabBarItem.badgeValue; max 3 digits + '+'; red circle top-right of icon
Android Convention: Badge on Navigation Bar destination; MaterialBadge; optional text badge or dot
Cross-Platform Note: React Navigation tabBarBadge; Flutter Badge widget (Material 3)
Accessibility: Badge values announced with tab label: '3 unread, Inbox tab'; badge-only dots say 'new content'

Keywords: master detail list select
When to Use: Viewing items in a list and their detail; email news reader; contacts
When to Avoid: When items have no meaningful detail; single-item apps; dynamic content with no list
iOS Convention: UISplitViewController (iPad); collapses to NavigationController on iPhone
Android Convention: Adaptive layout: list + detail pane on large; single pane on phone
Cross-Platform Note: Flutter responsive layout NavigationRail + detail; React Navigation split
Accessibility: List items have accessible names; detail pane announced when updated; loading states announced

Keywords: fab floating action button primary action navigate
When to Use: Launching creation flow (new message new post); single most important action
When to Avoid: Showing multiple options (use bottom sheet or menu); navigation between peer sections
iOS Convention: Not standard iOS — use large primary button or toolbar button
Android Convention: FAB (MD3); bottom-right position; morphs to small/medium/large
Cross-Platform Note: React Native FAB; Flutter FloatingActionButton; Compose FloatingActionButton
Accessibility: FAB must have accessibilityLabel (not just icon); tooltip on long press describes action; single FAB per screen

Keywords: onboarding wizard step flow multi-step
When to Use: First-time user setup; feature discovery; permission request sequence
When to Avoid: Returning users who completed onboarding; optional features
iOS Convention: PageViewController with progress indicator; 'Get Started' CTA; skip option
Android Convention: Compose onboarding with AnimatedVisibility; step indicator; 'Continue' / 'Skip' actions
Cross-Platform Note: React Navigation onboarding stack; Flutter onboarding page flow; stack with no back
Accessibility: Skip button must be accessible; step number announced ('Step 2 of 4'); 'Finish' announces completion

Keywords: back navigation ios swipe gesture return
When to Use: Returning to previous screen; navigating up in hierarchy
When to Avoid: Exiting modal flows (use dismiss/done); custom transitions that override system gesture
iOS Convention: UINavigationController back swipe; 'Back' label with previous screen title; left edge swipe
Android Convention: Not applicable on iOS — Android uses different pattern
Cross-Platform Note: React Navigation iOS uses swipe-back by default; cannot fully prevent system gesture
Accessibility: Back button must have accessible label; if custom back hidden — add explicit close/done button

Keywords: back button android system gesture navigation
When to Use: Returning in back stack; Android predictive back gesture (API 34+)
When to Avoid: Inconsistent with forward navigation direction; clearing back stack unexpectedly
iOS Convention: Not applicable on Android
Android Convention: System back button/gesture; predictive back animation (API34+); onBackPressed override
Cross-Platform Note: React Native BackHandler; Flutter Navigator pop; Compose BackHandler
Accessibility: Back gesture is system-level accessible; add software back button if gesture may not be discoverable

Keywords: expandable list accordion sections expand collapse
When to Use: Grouped settings with subcategories; categorized content; FAQs
When to Avoid: Deep hierarchical navigation (prefer push nav); if items have detail views (push instead)
iOS Convention: DisclosureGroup (SwiftUI); expandable UITableView sections
Android Convention: ExpandableListView; LazyColumn with expand state in Compose
Cross-Platform Note: React Native Accordion; Flutter ExpansionTile; AccordionSection
Accessibility: Announce expanded/collapsed state; 'expanded' or 'collapsed' appended to section header

Keywords: quick action home screen shortcut 3d touch long press
When to Use: Common app actions from home screen; power-user shortcuts
When to Avoid: Actions requiring authentication; destructive irreversible actions
iOS Convention: UIApplicationShortcutItem (Home screen); UIContextMenuInteraction (in-app peek)
Android Convention: Android shortcuts (static + dynamic); App manifest shortcut; pinned shortcuts
Cross-Platform Note: React Native (iOS only quickActions); not available in Flutter for Android without native code
Accessibility: Each shortcut must have a title; avoid misleading action names; confirm destructive shortcuts

Keywords: search results list filter navigate
When to Use: Displaying filtered content; showing search suggestions; navigating to result items
When to Avoid: Empty states without messaging; results without back-to-search path
iOS Convention: UISearchController resultsController; push result detail on tap; clear returns to search
Android Convention: Search results in RecyclerView; tap to navigate; back returns to search with query preserved
Cross-Platform Note: React Native FlatList with search results; Flutter SearchDelegate buildResults
Accessibility: Announce result count; 'No results' announced; result items have descriptive accessible names

Keywords: tab switcher browser-like multiple instances
When to Use: Managing multiple open items (browser tabs); multi-window workflow
When to Avoid: Standard single-flow apps; creates cognitive overhead; use sparingly
iOS Convention: UITabOverview; Safari-style grid; SwiftUI approach varies
Android Convention: Chrome Custom Tabs; multi-window Android 12+ split-screen
Cross-Platform Note: React Native WebView tab pattern; Flutter WebView with tab management
Accessibility: Tab count announced; active tab identified; close tab buttons labeled with tab title

Keywords: popup menu dropdown anchor contextual
When to Use: Selecting from 2-6 options anchored to element; sort options; filter options; secondary actions on item
When to Avoid: Long lists (use full sheet); primary action (use button); permanent choices
iOS Convention: UIMenu (iOS 14+); anchored popover on iPad; action sheet on iPhone
Android Convention: DropdownMenu (MD3) anchored popover; dismisses on outside tap or selection
Cross-Platform Note: React Native Modal with styled menu; @react-native-menu/menu; Flutter PopupMenuButton
Accessibility: Menu items announced; checkmarks in menu read as 'selected'; dismiss on Escape/back

Keywords: share sheet system share native ios android
When to Use: Sharing content to other apps or contacts; exporting data
When to Avoid: App-internal sharing (show inline options instead); if only 1 destination
iOS Convention: UIActivityViewController; system sheet; cannot customize activity order
Android Convention: Android Sharesheet (Intent.ACTION_SEND); ChooserIntent; recent contacts shown
Cross-Platform Note: React Native Share API; Flutter Share plugin; both trigger native system sheet
Accessibility: Native share sheets are fully accessible; custom share UIs must provide same actions with labels
